title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
In a drawing room, Louisa Wallace and Hercule Poirot examine a painting. Louisa expresses distress over the conspicuous blue pots and bowls depicted in the work, noting that they were once present in her house.

Later, at his apartment, Poirot requests paper and pencils from Mrs. Blanche Oncleworth. He prepares an envelope containing a drawing and a letter, with instructions for Mrs. Oncleworth to have Scotland Yard deliver it immediately to Inspector Stanley Bill regarding the murder at the Browns Hotel. While Mrs. Oncleworth makes the call, she receives news of another murder occurring at the Ritz Hotel.

In a separate sequence, an unnamed narrator visits "The Happy Coffee House" in London and speaks with a waitress named Phyllis Spurling, nicknamed "Pompadour Head." Phyllis discusses her desire to rename the establishment "The Happy Tea Room" and shares observations regarding Jenny Hobbs. Phyllis recalls a night when a woman named Miss Havisham visited, during which Jenny Hobbs rushed to the window to stare at Phyllis's reflection in the glass. Phyllis felt that Jenny was watching her intently, as if Phyllis herself were the dangerous one.

After the narrator visits Scotland Yard, they return to the apartment to find Poirot appearing uncharacteristically anxious and flushed as he prepares to leave. Mrs. Oncleworth is also acting strangely, appearing flushed and complaining about a delayed car.
